events.title: 2026 Needham High School Civics Showcase
events.description:
Event date: May 30, 2026 
Event Time: 02:00 PM - 03:30 PM
Location: 
1471 Highland Avenue
Needham, MA 02492
Description:
The League of Women Voters of Needham and the Needham High School Social Studies Department are co-sponsoring Students Advocate for Change, the Needham High School Civics Showcase, on Saturday, May 30 at 2-3:30 PM at Powers Hall, Needham Town Hall.All high school students in MA are required to select a state or local issue and prepare an advocacy plan. At the Showcase, more than 100 eleventh grade students in 50 groups will share their research, materials, and positions in a science fair type setting.The public is encouraged to come and talk with the next generation of policy advocates about the issues important to them. Visit www.lwv-needham.org for details.

events.title: Memorial Day Parade and Ceremony
events.description: Parade route and schedule 8:00-8:15am—Parade formation on South Ave., between Main Street and Washington St. 8:30am—Flag Ceremony at Moran Square at the Natick Center T-Station 8:45am—Parade marches to and around the Town Common Observances on the Common 9am Opening remarks at the Common John T. Martin, Veterans Services Officer Benediction- Chaplain, NSSC “National Anthem” by Natick High School Band Remarks from State Senator Karen Spilka’s office Remarks from State Representative David Linsky Remarks from Select Board Chair—Kathryn Coughlin Placing wreath at the Grand Army of Republic Monument by Scouts Keynote Speaker—Dave Josselyn, Vietnam Veteran “America the Beautiful” performed by Natick High Chorus Students “Taps” performed by Natick High School Band Parade forms up in front of the Common and marches: From East Central St. to West Central St. to Forest Ave. to Pond St. Parade marches and stops along the route for moment of silence and Taps, in honor of the fallen service members at: Old Dell Park Cemetery (163 Pond St.) St. Patrick’s Cemetery (180 Pond St.) Parade marches back to center of town and disbands All Veterans and Active Military are welcome to march in the parade. Please be advised that road closures will be in effect to accommodate parade traffic. In case of inclement weather, updates will be posted on the town website, and a decision regarding the parade will be made by 7am. If the parade needs to be moved indoors, the indoor location will be at the Natick Community Senior Center, located at 117 East Central St., Natick, at 9am. Thank you to Veterans Services Officer John Martin for this information.

events.title: American Revolution reenactment at Needham Town Common
events.description: For the third straight year, one of New England’s leading reenacting units, The King’s Own (4th Foot), is bringing its mix of history and fun to Needham Town Common May 30, 9 am-1 pm. See Redcoats drilling and preparing for war. Learn about equipment, organization, tactics, and weapons. Come face to face with soldiers sent to prevent or stop the American Revolution, and participate in an interactive debate on independence. Kids will have the chance to learn to march and handle (child safe!) muskets. Sponsored by the Needham Council for Arts and Culture.

events.title: King’s Own Come to Needham!
events.description: The British Are Coming! On Saturday, May 30th Needham will once again host The King’s Own, a group of reenactors who portray the British Redcoats who sought to prevent the American Revolution. Adults, teens, and children are invited to attend the fun, educational event that helps commemorate the 250th Anniversary of the founding of our country. See authentic uniforms, weapons, and drill, ask questions, and learn about the challenging days that led ordinary people of Needham into direct confrontation with their King and his army. Learn how British soldiers lived, fought, and made their way though seven years of warfare from Canada to the Caribbean. This event is sponsored by the Needham Cultural Council and is free to all.

events.title: King’s Own Come to Needham!
events.description: @ The King’s Own, a group of reenactors of the British Army in 1775, will once again return to Needham for a day of interactive learning. This year is the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence. See Redcoats at drill, learn what life was like in the 18th century, and dive into Needham’s history in an interactive event that should be fun and engaging for both adults and children. Sponsored by the Needham Culture and Arts Council. Add to calendar Google Calendar iCalendar Outlook 365 Outlook Live Details Date: Time:

events.title: Hunnewell Estate Historical Tour
events.description: Saturday, May 30, 2026, 9:45 AM-12:00 PM The Tollhouse Shop, 229 Washington Street, Wellesley, MA, 02481 Space is limited! We have 24 spaces available per tour. Tickets here. Experience the rich history and vibrant present of the Hunnewell Estate with the Wellesley Historical Society’s exclusive walking tour. This 90-minute journey delves into the estate’s unique horticultural collection, offering insights into both its past and present. With only 24 spaces available per tour, this intimate experience ensures a detailed exploration of the gardens. Please note that the tour includes uneven terrain and requires all participants to use the provided transportation. Proceeds from the tour will benefit the Society’s general operating fund, supporting our mission to preserve local history.

events.title: Free movie screening of “Turning Red” at TCAN
events.description: Movie Screening: May 30th, 11am, at TCAN In celebration of Mental Health Awareness Month, the Natick Health Department is hosting a free screening of Turning Red at the TCAN Cinema Room. Turning Red introduces Mei Lee, a confident, dorky 13-year-old torn between staying her mother’s dutiful daughter and the chaos of adolescence. Her protective, if not slightly overbearing mother, Ming, is never far from her daughter—an unfortunate reality for the teenager. And as if changes to her interests, relationships and body weren’t enough, whenever she gets too excited (which is practically ALWAYS), she “poofs” into a giant red panda! Visit: turningred.com Location: 14 Summer St, Natick, MA 01760 The Center For Arts In Natick @11:00AM Register Here: https://tcan.org/events/turning-red/

events.title: Natick Garden Club Plant Sale
events.description: The Natick Garden Club is hosting its annual plant sale on Sunday, May 31 from 10am-2pm indoors at the Natick Community-Senior Center, 117 East Central St. The sale is the group’s major fundraiser for the year. They offer a variety of plants that have been proven to grow successfully in our sometimes challenging weather conditions. Most of the plants are perennials from members’ gardens, and are labeled for bloom time and season, height, spacing needed, and sun and moisture requirements. They promote native plants as well, meaning they are native to our area, and tolerant of drought conditions and excellent at providing habitat for good insects and birds. There will be a modest houseplant and seedling section. Raffle tickets for a variety of items will be available and you don’t need to be present to win. Stay in the loop by signing up for Natick Report‘s free weekday email newsletter

events.title: Craft with Wellesley Middle School Girl Scouts
events.description: Craft with Middle School Girl Scouts Sunday, May 31, 4:30 – 5:30 pm 5th – 7th graders snack & craft Adult orientation at 5:15 pm RSVP or ask questions: gstroop73505@gmail.com Fun & Friendship for Middle School Girls Wellesley Girl Scouts for 6th–8th graders combines fun, friendship, and adventure with community service. Scouts may also choose to pursue their Silver Award, a girl-led “take action” project which improves their community. Our troop meets monthly on Sundays late afternoon with one additional weekend activity scheduled each month. Participation is flexible based on your scout’s interests and schedule; our members attend various schools.This year’s activities have included: Community Service: archery and ice skating for younger girls, holiday party for Wellesley senior residents, and collecting food for Wellesley food Pantry. Scouts earned their animal helper badge by learning about the animals Unity Farm Sanctuary and crafting toys for Buddy Dog and Stray Pets in Need. Outings: apple picking, night climbing for Halloween, skiing at Wachusett, and back yard winter camping. The troop attended International Women’s Day at Mass Bay. We enjoyed the penguins at New England Aquarium and cannolis at Bova’s Bakery. The troop also bonded at beach volleyball, an arcade escape room, and cheered the Boston Legacy women’s soccer team to victory at Gillette Stadium. We will finish the year with a trip to Canobie Lake Park funded by our cookie sales. To RSVP or ask questions, please email gstroop73505@gmail.com Wellesley Girl Scouts are also recruiting girls at all grade levels (K-12) to form new troops or join existing troops in the fall. If you’re interested in joining Girl Scouts, please contact wellesleygs@gmail.com or customercare@gsema.org

events.title: 50 Hikes Through History
events.description: In Massachusetts, many parks and trails have been carved out of historical sites. Author Alison O’Leary, a journalist and coauthor of the Appalachian Mountain Club’s guidebook, Best Day Hikes Near Boston, shares her favorites through this hour-long program. Using maps and historic photos, O’Leary describes several day trip destinations with historical significance and interesting features. events.description: Join the Wellesley Trails Committee on a guided trail walk. Free. No registration required. Walks take place unless it pours rain or there are high winds. Dogs on leashes are welcome. DATE: Saturday, June 6, 2026 TIME: 10:00am-12:30pm LOCATION: Meet at the Longfellow Pond parking lot at 220 Oakland Street DESCRIPTION: Mini-Grand Tour (6.5 miles) Join us for a 6.5-mile, 2.5+ hour Mini-Grand Tour that links a selection of our favorite trails, with a few sidewalks in between. From Longfellow Pond, we will find our way to Centennial Reservation, the Crosstown Trail at Indian Springs Park, John G. Schuler Path, Hunnewell Fyffe footbridge, Charles River Path, Oullet Park, and Barton Road trail, before returning to the Town Forest. While not as long as the Grand Tour, the Mini Tour is more strenuous than our typical walk. Remember to bring water and wear sturdy walking shoes. Meet at the Longfellow Pond parking lot at 220 Oakland Street.

events.title: “Regenerative Gardening in Today’s World”
events.description: @ Trevor Smith, an award-winning regenerative landscape designer and a principal of CFS Regenerative Design Studios, will discuss practices that climate change has brought to home gardening: planting native species to preserve biodiversity, combatting invasive species, building healthy soils, installing green infrastructure and managing water. The program will follow a very brief Friends’ Annual Meeting on June 7, 2026 at 2:00 p.m. at the Needham Free Public Library. Mr. Smith is the founder of ReEarth Solutions, a Boston-area ecological landscape design practice. He is passionate about the natural world which inspires his commitment to sustainable landscaping practices in an era of climate change. He is also passionate about sharing his knowledge and experience and is the host of the podcast “Peace, Love & Pollinators;” the live, online, “ask the expert” event Tuesdays with Trevor; and the Hive-Mind for Business Owners, a program of the Ecological Landscape Alliance. The public is invited to this free program presented by the Friends of the Needham Public Library in the Library’s Community Room at 1139 Highland Avenue, Needham, from 2-3:30 PM, Sunday, June 7. A question-and answer period will follow the presentation. Seats are limited and registration is required. Register at https://needhamlibrary.org/events/. For more information, please contact Gay Ellen Dennett, program specialist, at gdennett@minlib.net, 781-455-7559 x223. events.title: All Aboard, an accessible and adaptive cycling program on Cochituate Rail Trail
events.description: The Town of Natick presents All Aboard, an accessible and adaptive cycling program. Anyone that has a mobility limitation and wants to experience cycling is welcome and encouraged to participate. The Friends of Natick Trails supports the All Aboard program. To register as a rider, click the link below: June 13, 2026 Time slots: 10am-3pm Meeting spot: 11 Mechanic St., Natick, MA 01760 Natick is partnering with All Out Adventures, who brings a fleet of adaptive and accessible tricycles and bicycles to Natick on scheduled Saturdays. Staff will ensure a proper fit, and volunteers are available to accompany riders. All rides will start at the Mechanic Street lot on the Cochituate Rail Trail. This program is free to riders. Please note that advance registration is required for riders and volunteers.

events.title: Wellesley Trails Committee Guided Walk—Centennial Reservation
events.description: Join the Wellesley Trails Committee on a guided trail walk. Free. No registration required. Walks take place unless it pours rain or there are high winds. Dogs on leashes are welcome. DATE: Saturday, June 13 TIME: 10am-11:15am LOCATION: Meet at the Mass Bay parking lot off of Oakland Street, (not the Centennial lot) DESCRIPTION: Centennial Reservation (1.8 miles). Take a picturesque walk, through rolling meadows to the top of Maugus Hill, one of the highest points in Wellesley. View the beautiful Great Blue Hills in Canton from a hillside meadow overlooking the town of Needham. Then, descend through the meadow and around Benzanson Pond, a favorite swimming hole for dogs as well as frogs and tadpoles. Come explore this conservation land purchased by the town for Wellesley’s 1981 Centennial Celebration and now one of the most popular trails in Wellesley. This walk also includes Mass Bay land that is maintained by the Wellesley Trails Committee and the Wellesley DPW.

events.title: All Aboard, an accessible and adaptive cycling program on Cochituate Rail Trail
events.description: The Town of Natick presents All Aboard, an accessible and adaptive cycling program. Anyone that has a mobility limitation and wants to experience cycling is welcome and encouraged to participate. The Friends of Natick Trails supports the All Aboard program. To register as a rider, click the link below: September 26, 2026 Time slots: 10am-3pm Meeting spot: 11 Mechanic St., Natick, MA 01760 Natick is partnering with All Out Adventures, who brings a fleet of adaptive and accessible tricycles and bicycles to Natick on scheduled Saturdays. Staff will ensure a proper fit, and volunteers are available to accompany riders. All rides will start at the Mechanic Street lot on the Cochituate Rail Trail. This program is free to riders. Please note that advance registration is required for riders and volunteers.

events.title: Call for Walkers: Register for the 38th Annual Jimmy Fund Walk for Dana-Farber presented by Hyundai
events.description: Registration is open for the 2026 Jimmy Fund Walk for Dana-Farber presented by Hyundai. Scheduled for Sunday, Oct. 4, funds raised from the Walk support all forms of adult and pediatric care and cancer research at Dana-Farber Cancer Institute. The 2026 event will aim to raise $10 million in support of Dana-Farber’s mission to defy cancer by leading the way in groundbreaking research and lifesaving patient care. The Details: The Jimmy Fund Walk is the only organized walk permitted to use the famed Boston Marathon® course, and participants have the flexibility to choose from four distance options: 5K walk: Starting at Dana-Farber’s Longwood Medical Campus 10K walk: Starting in Newton Half Marathon walk: Starting in Wellesley Marathon walk: Starting in Hopkinton Along the route, participants will be motivated by Hero posters, photos and quotes from Dana-Farber patients, who are considered the heart and inspiration for the event. Walkers will also enjoy refreshments and cheer squads throughout the course. The event will conclude at the Boston Common Finish Line, by the corner of Charles and Beacon Streets. Finish line activities will include a celebration with food, entertainment, and more. Walkers who prefer to participate virtually can complete their chosen distance from any location. To register for the Jimmy Fund Walk for Dana-Farber or to support a walker, visit JimmyFund.org/Walk or call (866) 531-9255. Registrants can enter the promo code NEWS for $5 off the registration fee. You can also sign up to volunteer on Walk Day.

events.title: Needham High School Artisan Craft Fair and Bake Sale
events.description: The 43nd Annual Needham High School Craft Fair will be taking place on Saturday, October 24, 2026, from 9 am to 3 pm at Needham High School. Come shop from over 100 Local Vendors featuring affordable, high quality unique items, and from our incredible Bake Sale with homemade favorites prepared by our wonderful community, plus donations from local restaurants & bakeries. All proceeds benefit Needham High School PTC programs that support NHS students throughout the year. For more information, please email needhamcraftfair@gmail.com
